definition:
μακράν (prop. fem. accusative of μακρός, Sc. ὁδόν), adv. ,
 [in LXX for רָחוֹק hi., רָחַק, etc. ;] 
a long way, far: Luk.15:20, Act.22:21; before ἀπό, Mat.8:30, Luk.7:6, Jhn.21:8, Act.17:27; id. metaphorically, Mrk.12:34; οἱ εἰς μ., Act.2:39 (cf. Isa.2:2); metaphorically, οἱ ποτὲ ὄντες μ. (opposite to ἐγγύς), Eph.2:13; οἱ μ., Eph.2:17.†
 (AS)
